abstract: Please join the session that is best suited to your time zone...this topic is repeated at 2 different times. Generative AI has emerged as the driving force of a new era, reshaping our work processes and transforming the content creations landscape. However, there are a lot of misconceptions about how it works. In this presentation, we’ll cover what Generative AI really is, what it can and can't do, and the potential impacts it can have on content creators and the publishing industry. We’ll explore how content creators can identify whether their work has been sampled, get compensation if their content has been used, and protect their work from being sampled by AI tools. We’ll also discuss whether using AI tools in your work is "cheating" or a legitimate tool in helping to draft your work. Through real-time demonstrations, Rachel will demystify Generative AI and teach us how to use the tools. Bring your questions and let’s learn together! Presenter: Rachel Miles Rachel is a user experience researcher. A self-proclaimed nerd of all trades, she loves to learn about everything that crosses her path. In her spare time, you might catch her reading, drawing, traveling, or working or working on her blog where she talks about where technology meets wellness. Blog: https://renaissancerachel.com LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/rachelmiles/ \ \ \ Please join us at the session that is best suited to your time zone. Note that this topic is: 1\. Repeated at two different times to accommodate various time zones\, because it is 2\. Posted simultaneously in multiple meetup groups world\-wide \ \ \ It is recommended that you register at this Webex link ahead of time to receive a calendar invite and reminder. https://ibm.webex.com/weblink/register/rf4fe45cac6dbe70bd9065740ed8e6d71
event_title: Navigating Generative AI - what content creators need to know
title: Navigating Generative AI - what content creators need to know
